report_name: Belarus-Aligned APT 'FrostyNeighbor' Deploys New JavaScript Loader in Attacks on Poland & Ukraine

threat_actors.name: FrostyNeighbor
threat_actors.description: A Belarus-aligned, state-sponsored cyber-espionage group that targets government and military organizations in Poland and Ukraine. The group is known for information operations and has evolved its toolset to include a new JavaScript-based loader.
threat_actors.goals: Cyber-espionage and information operations, including long-term intelligence gathering and theft of classified information.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: The campaign initiates with spear-phishing emails containing PDF lures. These PDFs include links that direct victims to attacker-controlled infrastructure.

attack_patterns.name: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: Blurry PDF attachments were used as lures, containing malicious links to entice users to click.

attack_patterns.name: Environmental Keying
attack_patterns.description: The attacker's server performs server-side victim validation and fingerprinting, only delivering the final payload if the system matches a desired target profile and does not appear to be a sandbox or researcher's machine.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: JavaScript
attack_patterns.description: A new version of the PicassoLoader malware was developed using JavaScript, likely to evade signature-based detection.

attack_patterns.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
attack_patterns.description: The PicassoLoader downloader is used to fetch and execute the next stage of the attack, the Cobalt Strike beacon.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: Cobalt Strike loaders often utilize PowerShell for execution within a compromised network.

attack_patterns.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
attack_patterns.description: Cobalt Strike, the final payload, is a post-exploitation framework known for using obfuscation to evade defenses.

malware.name: PicassoLoader
malware.description: A JavaScript-based downloader used by FrostyNeighbor. It is delivered to validated targets and is responsible for fetching and executing the Cobalt Strike beacon.

tools.name: Cobalt Strike
tools.description: The final payload deployed by PicassoLoader. It is a post-exploitation framework used for lateral movement, credential theft, data exfiltration, and long-term espionage.

identities.name: Government organizations in Poland
identities.description: A primary target sector for the FrostyNeighbor cyber-espionage campaign.

identities.name: Military organizations in Ukraine
identities.description: A primary target sector for the FrostyNeighbor cyber-espionage campaign.

identities.name: Ukrtelecom
identities.description: Ukrainian telecommunications provider whose brand was impersonated in the PDF phishing lures.

infrastructure.name: FrostyNeighbor C2 and Payload Delivery Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Attacker-controlled infrastructure used to host malicious links, perform server-side victim validation, and deliver the PicassoLoader and Cobalt Strike payloads.

indicators.name: Suspicious Wscript/Cscript Execution of Downloaded JavaScript
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Wscript/Cscript Execution of JavaScript
id: 2b8f1c9d-5e6a-4b3c-8a7d-9e8f7a6b5c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of JavaScript files (.js) by wscript.exe or cscript.exe, which is a TTP used by FrostyNeighbor to run their PicassoLoader.
references:
  - https://www.darkreading.com/apt-groups/frostyneighbor-apt-carefully-targets-govt-orgs-in-poland-ukraine
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-15
tags:
  - attack.t1059.007
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_img:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
  selection_cli:
    CommandLine|contains: '.js'
  condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative scripts.
indicators.description: Detects the execution of JavaScript files by wscript.exe or cscript.exe, a technique used by FrostyNeighbor to execute the PicassoLoader malware.

indicators.name: PowerShell Web Download Cradle
indicators.pattern:
title: PowerShell Web Download Cradle
id: 8c4d5e6f-7a8b-4c3d-9e8f-7a6b5c4d3e2f
status: experimental
description: Detects a common PowerShell download cradle pattern (IEX(New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadString), often used by frameworks like Cobalt Strike for payload delivery.
references:
  - https://www.darkreading.com/apt-groups/frostyneighbor-apt-carefully-targets-govt-orgs-in-poland-ukraine
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-15
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1105
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'IEX'
      - 'New-Object'
      - 'Net.WebClient'
      - 'DownloadString'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate but rare use by administration scripts.
indicators.description: Detects the use of a common PowerShell download cradle, a technique associated with Cobalt Strike loaders.

relationships.target_name: FrostyNeighbor C2 and Payload Delivery Infrastructure
FrostyNeighbor C2 and Payload Delivery Infrastructure

relationships.source_name: Suspicious Wscript/Cscript Execution of Downloaded JavaScript
